list.Title: Res. #1E-202605-06:  Requesting Expedited Traffic Safety Review for Park Place NW Between Princeton Place NW and Otis Place NW
list.Description: ANC 1E requests that DDOT expedite review of traffic safety concerns on Park Place NW between Princeton Place NW and Otis Place NW, including dangerous speeding, crashes, hit-and-runs, and related public-safety concerns. The resolution asks DDOT to evaluate feasible safety treatments, including raised crosswalks, automated speed enforcement, driver feedback signs, pavement markings, daylighting, hardened centerlines, curb bulb-outs, and lane-narrowing options, and to provide a written update on the Traffic Safety Input review.

list.Title: Res. #1E-202605-05:  Calling for Improved Condominium Warranty Security Notification and Transparency
list.Description: ANC 1E adopted a resolution calling on DHCD to improve transparency and notice around condominium warranty security after The Exchange Condominium at 3619 Georgia Avenue NW experienced confusion and potential financial exposure related to a missing or delayed warranty bond. The resolution urges DHCD to notify condominium associations when warranty security is received, renewed, changed, or released; review past enforcement cases where associations may not have been notified; provide ANC 1E and the Council with an update on public access to warranty-security records; and work with relevant District partners to issue plain-language guidance for condominium associations and ANCs.

list.Title: Res. #1E-2026-02:  To approve the grant application for Lower Georgia Avenue Main Street’s Chili Cook-off
list.Description: ANC 1E approves a grant application from Lower Georgia Avenue Main Street to support its Chili Cook-off, a community event intended to promote neighborhood engagement, support local businesses, and benefit residents within the Commission area.

list.Title: Res. #1E-202605-03
list.Description: ANC 1E urges the Department of Health to approve a partnership between its Rodent and Vector Control Division and recognized international rodent-control experts Dr. Giovanna Massei and Dr. Jens Jacob. The resolution calls on DOH to work with these experts to improve the District’s rodent fertility-management pilot and ensure the program is scientifically valid for future use.

list.Title: RESOLUTION URGING FLEXIBILITY IN THE 350-FOOT SCHOOL BOUNDARY  CONSIDERATION FOR THE MURCH ELEMENTARY SAFROUTES TO SCHOOL PLAN
list.Description: ANC 3F urges DDOT to expand the scope of the Murch Elementary Safe Routes to School plan beyond the standard 350-foot boundary so that safety concerns along students’ actual travel routes are fully evaluated. In particular, the resolution asks DDOT to assess the high-crash intersection of Connecticut Avenue and Davenport Street NW and use both Vision Zero data and community feedback to guide safety improvements.

list.Title: Requesting DCHA Oversight related to Rent Reasonableness
list.Description: Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4B officially passed Resolution #4B-26-0502 to demand external oversight and programmatic accountability regarding the District of Columbia Housing Authority’s (DCHA) administration of the Housing Choice Voucher Program (HCVP). While ANC 4B unequivocally supports the mission of the HCVP to provide safe, dignified housing, a multi-year breakdown in the application of "Rent Reasonableness" standards has created severe market distortions and fiscal mismanagement on the 6300–6500 blocks of North Capitol Street, the unit blocks of Tuckerman Street and Underwood Place NE and NW, and the 6400 block of Kansas Avenue, NE. The resolution formally requests that the DC Office of the Inspector General (OIG) and the DCHA Office of Audit and Compliance conduct specific financial and structural investigations in order to address these longstanding payment discrepancies.

list.Title: Requesting DCHA Oversight related to Health, Safety, and Sanitation
list.Description: Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4B officially passed Resolution #4B-26-0503 to demand external oversight and programmatic accountability regarding the District of Columbia Housing Authority’s (DCHA) administration of the Housing Choice Voucher Program (HCVP). While ANC 4B unequivocally supports the mission of the HCVP to provide safe, dignified housing, a multi-year breakdown in landlord oversight and lease enforcement has created health, safety, and sanitation issues on the 6300–6500 blocks of North Capitol Street, the unit blocks of Tuckerman Street and Underwood Place, and the 6400 block of Kansas Avenue, NE. The resolution formally requests that the DC Office of the Inspector General (OIG) and the DCHA Office of Audit and Compliance conduct specific interventions in order to address these longstanding issues.

ANC 4E Request Regarding Relocation of Public Trash Receptacle
list.Description: ANC 4E formally requested that the Department of Public Works remove the public trash receptacle located at Georgia Avenue and Buchanan Street NW and relocate it to 4300 Arkansas Avenue NW near Upshur Pool and the basketball courts. The request followed ongoing concerns from residents and DPW regarding persistent misuse of the receptacle for household trash disposal, resulting in overflowing trash, rodent activity, foul odors, and litter impacts on nearby homes and public spaces. DPW staff also noted that the receptacle was frequently refilled immediately after collection, creating the appearance that the can was not being serviced. At its May 12, 2026 public meeting, ANC 4E voted unanimously, 5-0-0 with one Commissioner absent, to support the relocation request and work collaboratively with DPW on improved placement options.

list.Title: Resolution Regarding the Temporary Suspension of Street Sweeping in Construction Project Zones Until Completion in ANC 4E
list.Description: ANC 4E adopted a resolution requesting the temporary suspension of street sweeping operations in construction zones throughout Crestwood and 16th Street Heights until project completion. The Commission recognized that ongoing infrastructure projects have already created significant parking limitations, roadway disruptions, and inconveniences for residents. Requiring additional vehicle movement for street sweeping in active construction areas places an unnecessary burden on the community while also representing an inefficient use of Department of Public Works resources. The resolution calls for suspension of street sweeping on several affected blocks, including portions of Crittenden, Decatur, Emerson, Gallatin, Hamilton, Ingraham, Jefferson, Longfellow, and Montague Streets, as well as Colorado Avenue and Kennedy Street NW. ANC 4E also requested seven workdays’ notice before sweeping resumes. The resolution passed unanimously, 5-0-0.

7B-26-02 - Resolution Stating Conditions for Support of the Pennsylvania Ave SE & Minnesota Ave SE Intersection Safety Improvement Project
list.Description: Endorses five elements of DDOT's existing design and conditions ANC 7B's support for a revised design on eight requirements: preservation of 25th Street SE vehicle and bus access from eastbound Pennsylvania Avenue SE; rush hour bus lanes on Pennsylvania Avenue SE between Prout Street SE and 27th Street SE converting to parking off-peak; protected bicycle infrastructure throughout the project area; retention of Nicholson Street SE as a westbound one-way street; bus bulb-outs at two 25th Street SE stops; a bus shelter and trash receptacle at 27th Street SE and R Street SE; civic activation of L'Enfant Square park space; and pedestrian safety improvements at the I-295 interchange crosswalks. The resolution also calls for formal DDOT/Office of Planning coordination to align the project with the Pennsylvania Avenue SE Small Area Plan, continued WMATA coordination, a written DDOT response before any design is finalized, and community review before construction resumes.

Resolution Designate OANC as Agent Related to DIFS and PASS Accounting System Maintenance
list.Description: This resolution authorizes ANC 1A to designate the Office of Advisory Neighborhood Commissions (OANC) as its agent for maintaining the Commission’s supplier registration and payment information in the District’s financial systems, specifically the District Integrated Financial System (DIFS) and the Payment and Reporting System (PASS). It notes that ANC 1A receives quarterly allotments via ACH direct deposit and that supplier profiles must be kept current and renewed every three years to avoid disruption of payments. To streamline compliance and ensure uninterrupted funding, the resolution empowers OANC to complete supplier registration, account maintenance, and required three-year renewals, including ACH authorization documentation, and further authorizes the Commission Treasurer to work directly with OANC and provide any necessary information.

list.Title: Resolution Amending ANC 1A Community Grant Guidance to Increase Award Amounts and Remove Award Limitations
list.Description: This resolution amends ANC 1A’s Official Guidance on Community Grants to raise the maximum grant amounts for community events and to remove prior award limitations to better reflect rising event costs and support more robust neighborhood programming. Specifically, it updates Section III(D) so that small events with 100 or fewer attendees remain eligible for up to $500, medium events with 101–500 attendees may receive up to $1,000, and large events with 501 or more attendees may receive up to $3,000, while preserving the Commission’s ability to grant special exceptions with a three-quarters majority vote. The resolution strikes Section III(E) (“Award Allowance Limitations”) in its entirety, leaves all other provisions of the grant guidance unchanged, and encourages community organizations, civic groups, and residents to use the expanded funding levels to increase participation and strengthen neighborhood cohesion.

list.Title: Resolution Requesting Budget Allocation for Dog Waste Awareness Signage
list.Description: This ANC 1A resolution authorizes the allocation of up to $500 from the Commission’s budget to design, create, and purchase dog waste awareness signs.  These signs may be distributed to 1A residents for placement in their private yards and other appropriate, public locations throughout Columbia Heights where dog walking activity is common.  The purpose of these signs shall be to encourage residents and visitors to pick up and properly dispose of dog waste, reinforce community norms of shared responsibility for neighborhood cleanliness, and support ongoing efforts to improve sanitation and rodent control in the community.  ANC 1A urges the DOEE, DPW, and other relevant District agencies to expand the installation of pet waste bag dispensers and disposal stations in high-dog-traffic areas of Columbia Heights, increase the maintenance of public trash receptacles, and ensure that existing waste infrastructure is regularly monitored and serviced.

list.Title: Symbolically Designate the 5000 Block of Benning Road, SE as “Willie J. Hardy Way”
list.Description: This resolution honors the legacy of Willie J. Hardy, a pioneering Ward 7 leader, former DC Councilmember, and lifelong advocate for economic opportunity and community development. It recognizes her deep ties to the Benning Road corridor and her decades of service supporting District residents through public service and anti-poverty initiatives. The resolution calls for the symbolic designation of the 5000 block of Benning Road, SE as “Willie J. Hardy Way” to provide a visible and lasting tribute to her contributions.

list.Title: Equitable and Finance-Aware Housing Policy
list.Description: This resolution supports increasing housing production in the District while emphasizing that zoning changes alone are not sufficient to achieve equitable outcomes. It highlights the need to consider financing conditions, infrastructure capacity, and long-term stability to ensure housing growth is sustainable. The resolution raises concerns about disproportionate redevelopment pressure and concentration of affordable housing in lower-cost communities, including Ward 7. It calls for a more balanced, citywide approach that distributes housing growth and investment equitably, with strong safeguards, transparency, and alignment with infrastructure planning.

list.Title: Arizona Avenue Bridge and Trolley Trail Project
list.Description:
ANC 3D voted 7-0-0 to send this letter supporting, with concerns and recommendations, DDOT’s plan for the Arizona Avenue pedestrian bridge and the trolley trail from Galena Place to Nebraska Avenue. 

Our vision for this trail is that it will be used overwhelmingly by children, parents, and other residents to go to and from our Rec Center. It will be the safest place in our area for children to ride their bikes. Certainly, we do not want a lot of through-traffic and the kind of speed that occurs on the Capital Crescent Trail. We do not believe that kind of traffic will happen because (1) the trail is only 6/10ths of a mile long; (2) it will be narrow, only 8 feet; and (3) this stretch of trail only connects parts of our neighborhood, it is not a place that is attractive to through-traffic. Eight specific comments about the project are detailed in the letter.

list.Title: Resolution calling for immediate remediation of DC 311 web and mobile platform failures and standardization of service request intake
list.Description:
This resolution from ANC 5B calls on the District to urgently fix systemic failures in the DC 311 web portal and mobile app, which residents rely on to request city services. It documents real world breakdowns, including buggy mobile functionality, inconsistent service request options, poor mobile usability, and critical data not being transmitted to agencies like DPW, which delays response and enforcement.

The resolution urges the Office of Unified Communications, OCTO, and key service agencies to correct technical defects, standardize service request availability across all platforms, ensure complete and accurate data sharing, and improve transparency and accountability. It also calls on the DC Council to exercise stronger oversight to ensure the system works reliably for residents.

ANC 4D also urges the DCPSC to immediately revert rates to the most recent level prior to the 2024 multi-year rate plan adoption, and calls on the Mayor and Council to increase funding and benefit amounts for the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (“LIHEAP”) as more families fall behind, as well as pursue legislative opportunities to reduce rates for District residents.

list.Description: ANC 1E is urging the District to make future snow emergency response more fair, transparent, and pedestrian-safe by rotating delayed service routes equitably, improving public communications about route status and service suspensions, strengthening sidewalk and crosswalk access, clarifying snow removal enforcement, and deploying targeted cleanup resources to neighborhoods that experienced extended service gaps so affected blocks are restored to normal conditions as quickly as possible.

list.Description: ANC 3D supports the Field School's request to allow permitted construction work to occur at the school on weekends and holidays.

list.Title: Curb Cut Application 4403 W Street, N.W. PSRA Permit Tracking #484384
list.Description: Advisory Neighborhood Commission 3D voted to approve the curb cut application 5-0-0 for 4403 W Street, N.W. (PSRA Permit Tracking Number 484384 and Review Number 1018159). Approval is predicated on the condition that the owner place grass over the existing driveway on Foxhall Place leading to the rear of their home and pay to reinstall the curb where there is an existing curb cut. ANC 3D also notes the location already has a circular drive in close proximity to the intersection.

list.Title: FY2027 Funding for Horace Mann School
list.Description: Advisory Neighborhood Commission 3D voted 5-0-0 in support of Horace Mann Elementary’s (Mann) request to have their budget for FY 2027 maintained at a level equal to or greater than its FY2026 budget, thereby allowing the school to continue to serve its students adequately and maintain its record of excellence.

list.Title: #1E-2026-0201: LETTER SUPPORTING THE ROCK N’ ROLL HALF MARATHON
list.Description: ANC 1E supports the Rock N’ Roll DC Half Marathon special event application for March 21, 2026, including planned traffic mitigation and resident notification for route impacts in SMDs 1E05 and 1E07. The resolution designates Commissioner Josh Jacobson as the Commission’s representative and commits ANC 1E to help communicate temporary closures.

list.Title: #1E-2026-0205: URGING EXPANDED VOTER EDUCATION AND NEIGHBORHOOD OUTREACH ON RANKED- CHOICE VOTING AHEAD OF THE JUNE 2026 PRIMARY
list.Description: ANC 1E adopted a resolution urging the District to conduct robust voter education and outreach ahead of the June 2026 primary, when ranked-choice voting will be used for the first time in a high-turnout election. The resolution highlights the unusually competitive Ward 1 primary and the likelihood that many voters will encounter a ranked ballot for the first time. It calls on the District to ensure clear public communication, accessible educational materials, and targeted outreach so voters understand how to properly mark a ranked ballot and can participate confidently in the new system.

list.Description: ANC 3F passed a resolution in February 2026, that DDOT and Mayor Bowser approve the Connecticut Avenue Streetscape Project investment package and capital budget for this project in the 2027 budget cycle of at least $8-10 million (estimate) that will allow for the project to be completed as initially envisioned and designed.

list.Title: Resolution 4B-26-0201 Supporting the Pay our Youth a Fair Wage Amendment Act of 2025
list.Description:
•	The Commission strongly supports the Pay Our Youth A Fair Wage Summer Wage Amendment Act of 2025 and urges the Committee on Executive Administration and Labor to schedule a public hearing this legislation, to ensure that youth, families, employers, and community stakeholders can provide input on this important legislation.

•	ANC 4B encourages the District to pair wage increases with strong program support, including transportation assistance, financial literacy education, and expanded placement opportunities that reflect youth interests and career pathways and for DC Council to appropriate the necessary amount to fund these wage increases.

list.Title: ANC 4B Letter Regarding Animal Care and Control Contract Oversight
list.Description:
On behalf of Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4B, I am writing to transmit the attached letter titled “Questions Regarding Brandywine Valley SPCA (BVSPCA) and the District’s Animal Care and Control Contract”, adopted on February 23, 2026.
As outlined in the letter, the Commission has received multiple reports from District residents regarding the delivery of animal care and control services under the current contract, and we are seeking clarity on oversight, performance standards, and compliance. The questions included in the letter reflect both resident concerns and findings highlighted by the Office of the District of Columbia Auditor.
We request written responses to the 22 questions detailed in the attached letter. These responses will be shared at an upcoming ANC 4B public meeting to ensure transparency and support meaningful community oversight.
If you need any additional information, I can be reached at 4B06@anc.dc.gov.
Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. We look

list.Title: A Resolution Calling for Comprehensive Rat Mitigation Reform in the District Of Columbia
list.Description:
ANC 1A calls on the Mayor of the District of Columbia and the Council of the District of Columbia, and urges District agencies including the DC Health, DPW, and DOB, to take immediate, coordinated, and comprehensive action to reform the District’s rodent mitigation strategy and align policy, funding, and implementation with proven best practices. 

ANC 1A supports passage of the Rodent Abatement and Transparency (RAT) Amendment Act of 2025, recognizing its role in strengthening enforcement authority, interagency coordination, transparency through a public rodent abatement dashboard, and accountability for chronic noncompliance.  

ANC 1A supports passage and full funding of the Rodent Abatement Through Smart Solutions (RATSS) Amendment Act of 2025, recognizing that preventing rodent access to food sources through improved trash infrastructure is essential to long-term rodent mitigation.

list.Title: A Resolution Concerning the Installation of Anti-Slip Treatments on Metal Grating in Central Columbia Heights
list.Description:
ANC 1A requests that the District Department of Transportation, in coordination with relevant utility agencies, the Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Authority, and property owners, where applicable and necessary:

(1) Identify and assess metal grates and covers within sidewalks and pedestrian pathways in Central Columbia Heights that pose an elevated slip risk due to smooth metal grating or other considerations;

(2) Prioritize corrective action at locations with heavy pedestrian volume, including at DC USA, the area in front of 3101 14th St NW, and the associated WMATA Metrorail facility grates on the east and west sides of the street at the intersection of 14th Street and Harvard Street NW; and

(3) Replace existing smooth metal grating with non-skid or high-friction metal grating, or apply durable anti-slip treads or other high-friction surface treatments where full replacement is not immediately feasible.

list.Title: A Resolution Concerning Support for the Substantial Change Application of the Wonderland Ballroom
list.Description: ANC 1A supports the application for a substantial change in operations by The Wonderland Ballroom and finds no cause to object to, or protest said application, as the Applicant/Licensee has demonstrated appropriateness in accordance with D.C. Official Code §25-313, and no concerns were raised by the residents or businesses surrounding the Establishment.

list.Title: A Resolution Concerning Strengthening Compliance with and Enforcement of Micromobility Guidelines and Regulations in the District
list.Description: ANC 1A calls upon the District Department of Transportation to strengthen regulatory compliance and enforcement of all shared fleet device guidelines, permit operator agreement conditions, and regulations governing scooters, e-bikes, and similar devices within the neighborhood commission area of Columbia Heights, and across the District of Columbia.

list.Title: A Resolution Concerning Sidewalk Vending Improvements in Columbia Heights
list.Description:
ANC 1A represents a neighborhood commission area with significant amounts of sidewalk vending in public spaces and submits this resolution to promote policy that creates lawful vending opportunities,
strengthens day-to-day vending operations, and ensures equitable and consistent regulatory
compliance and enforcement.

list.Title: A Resolution Concerning Reinstallation of In-Street Pedestrian Crossing Signs on Lamont Street NW at 13th Street NW
list.Description:
ANC 1A requests that the District Department of Transportation reinstall in-street
pedestrian crossing signs at the centerline of Lamont Street NW at 13th Street NW, serving both
the northbound and southbound travel lanes.

list.Title: Request for Temporary Suspension of Street Sweeping Enforcement due to Ongoing Infrastructure Construction in the1400 Block of Longfellow Street NW and Adjacent Streets
list.Description: Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4E adopted a resolution requesting that the District Department of Public Works (DPW) continue the temporary suspension of street sweeping enforcement on the 1400 block of Longfellow Street NW and adjacent impacted streets due to ongoing infrastructure construction associated with the Lead Free DC initiative and related utility work. Construction activities have significantly reduced on-street parking availability and eliminated alley access for many residents, creating hardship—particularly for seniors and households without alternative parking options. DPW indicated it had no objection to continuing last season’s suspension while construction remains pending. The resolution formally memorializes ANC 4E’s request that enforcement remain suspended for the duration of active construction materially affecting parking access and requests timely notification of any changes in construction timelines that could affect enforcement status.

list.Title: Open TSIs in Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4B
list.Description: Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4B, at its duly noticed public meeting on November 24, 2025, with a quorum being the “majority of the total number of commissioner positions currently filled in Commission 4B,” voted with 7 Yeas, 0 Nays, and 0 Abstentions to send this letter to Director Kershbaum requesting a status update on the backlog of 28 prioritized traffic safety input investigations in 4B.

Resolution in Support of the One-Way Westbound Conversion of the 1300 Block of Farragut Street NW
list.Description: ANC 4E adopted a resolution supporting DDOT’s proposal to convert the 1300 block of Farragut Street NW to one-way westbound traffic. The change responds to documented congestion and safety concerns affecting John Lewis Elementary School operations and neighborhood traffic flow. Following DDOT evaluation and community engagement, the Commission confirmed strong constituent support for the conversion as a measure to improve pedestrian safety and traffic management. ANC 4E formally requests DDOT proceed expeditiously with the design phase and commits to ongoing community coordination throughout project implementation.

list.Title: ANC 4E Recommendation – 5760 - 5812 Georgia Avenue, NW and Old Engine Company 22—Public Surplus Property Use
list.Description: ANC 4E transmitted a formal recommendation to the Deputy Mayor for Planning and Economic Development regarding the future use of the publicly owned properties at 5760–5812 Georgia Avenue NW and Old Engine Company 22. Following a public surplus hearing and community engagement, the Commission voted unanimously to support redevelopment as a multi-use community hub anchored by a public library with dedicated ANC meeting space, a job training center for youth and adults, a child care facility, mixed-use housing and retail, and public open space. The Commission also urged consideration of rebuilding the Emery Recreation Center on the site to expand fields and playground areas, ensuring long-term community benefit and neighborhood revitalization.

list.Title: ANC 4E Request Regarding Residential Parking Parking During Citi Open 2025
list.Description: ANC 4E formally requested that the District expand temporary resident-only parking boundaries during the Mubadala Citi DC Open to address significant neighborhood parking strain. The tournament coincides with major infrastructure projects, including DC PLUG construction along 14th Street NW and ongoing alley and roadway work, compounding impacts on residents and small businesses in Crestwood and Sixteenth Street Heights. The Commission voted unanimously to urge the Mayor, DPW, and DDOT to extend enforcement east toward Georgia Avenue and south beyond current limits. The temporary expansion would help protect residential parking access, reduce congestion, and improve conditions for both residents and event visitors during the tournament period.

list.Title: Resolution Regarding the Proposed Transfer of Jurisdiction for Portions of Rock Creek Park (Subtitle N of the Fiscal Year 2026 Budget Support Act of 2025)
list.Description: ANC 4E adopted a resolution expressing concern over Subtitle N of the FY2026 Budget Support Act, which would transfer jurisdiction of portions of Rock Creek Park, including U.S. Reservation 339 and the Rock Creek Tennis Center, from the National Park Service to the District and authorize a $9 million capital project. While acknowledging potential benefits of local control and improved recreational amenities, the Commission objects to the lack of prior community engagement. ANC 4E urges the D.C. Council to delay action until meaningful consultation occurs and requests detailed information on project scope, boundaries, partnerships, timelines, and impacts on park access, traffic, parking, noise, and the future of Carter Barron Amphitheater.

list.Title: Resolution Requesting for Temporary Suspension of Residential Parking Permit Enforcement Due to the DC Plug Infrastructure Project in ANC 4E
list.Description: ANC 4E adopted a resolution requesting the temporary suspension of Residential Parking Permit (RPP) enforcement due to the DC PLUG/DDOT infrastructure project along the 4600–4700 blocks of 14th Street NW. The project eliminated paid parking spaces, creating significant hardship for local businesses, employees, and customers. The Commission seeks immediate relief from RPP infractions within defined boundaries between Delafield and Buchanan Streets NW and 14th and 16th Streets NW. The suspension would remain in effect through restoration of normal parking conditions, but no later than August 31, 2025, with written confirmation requested from DPW and coordination with DDOT and project managers.

list.Title: Request for Temporary Suspension of Residential Parking Permit Enforcement and Street Cleaning Due to Alley Construction in ACE 4E
list.Description: ANC 4E adopted a resolution requesting temporary suspension of Residential Parking Permit (RPP) and street cleaning enforcement during alley construction between Allison, Buchanan, 15th, and 16th Streets NW  ?. The permeable pavement project will close alley access, restrict parking, and disrupt trash and recycling placement, creating hardship for residents—particularly those without alternative parking or with accessibility needs. The Commission urges DPW, in coordination with DC Water and DDOT, to suspend enforcement for the full duration of construction and provide notice of any timeline changes to minimize unnecessary fines and stress during this city infrastructure upgrade.

list.Title: Request an Overview of the Proposed Design and Anticipated Impact of the Bus Stop Seating Project Installation at 14th & Longfellow Street, NW
list.Description: ANC 4E formally requested a briefing from WMATA and DDOT regarding the proposed Bus Stop Seating Project installation at 14th & Longfellow Street, NW.  While supportive of efforts to improve accessibility and rider comfort, the Commission expressed concern about limited engagement and the lack of a design presentation for this high-visibility transit location. ANC 4E sought renderings, installation timelines, construction impacts, and an explanation of accessibility and public feedback considerations. Pending such a briefing, the Commission subsequently voted to support the Public Space Application after learning about safety, accessibility, and design.

list.Title: Resolution Opposing the Missouri Avenue Segment of the Proposed Bike Lane Project and Requesting Reevaluation and Alternatives
list.Description: ANC 4E adopted a resolution opposing DDOT’s proposed Missouri Avenue NW bike lane as currently designed, citing significant safety, environmental, accessibility, and property concerns  ?. The Commission received substantial resident feedback regarding limited roadway width, speeding, bus operations, tree removal, retaining wall impacts, and loss of essential on-street parking—particularly affecting seniors and residents with mobility challenges. ANC 4E calls for a full environmental and traffic safety analysis, ADA and disparate impact review, and meaningful community engagement. The Commission urges DDOT to reevaluate alternative routes or shared-road models that achieve multimodal goals without disproportionate neighborhood impacts.

list.Title: Resolution Regarding Notice of Intent 25-285-TPA and  Enforcement Credibility in ANC 4E's Commercial Corridors
list.Description: ANC 4E submitted formal comments regarding DDOT Notice of Intent 25-285-TPA, which proposes expanding pay-to-park regulations on the 4400 block of 14th Street NW. While taking no position on the specific proposal, ANC 4E raises serious concerns about enforcement credibility in its commercial corridors. Businesses on the 4600 and 4700 blocks are experiencing significant economic harm due to contractor vehicles occupying patron parking during the Northern Bus Garage reconstruction. ANC 4E calls for immediate enforcement action, coordinated parking protocols with WMATA and contractors, regular reporting, and a formal accountability meeting before any further enforcement expansion is considered

list.Title: #1E-2025-1203 RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE DDOT’S COLUMBIA HEIGHTS CROSSTOWN BUS PRIORITY PROJECT W/ CURBSIDE BUS LANE (OPTION 1)
list.Description:
ANC1E selects Option1/Scenario 1, which involves a curbside bus lane for the full length of
the ANC1E corridors of Irving Street and Columbia Road NW, which will operate from 7am-7pm on weekdays, allowing residents to park overnight while ridership is down. ANC1E recognizes the benefits of selecting Option 1/Scenario 1, preserving the parking lane as a traffic calming measure (as previously studied by DDOT) during off-peak hours while bus ridership is low and speed and reckless driving tend to be high. ANC1E welcomes DDOT proposed designs before the impacted ANCs at 30%, 60% and 90% prior to the rollout of the final plans and strongly encourages a phased community input process for ANCs to weigh in on DDOT’s project offerings including safety, sidewalk and bus stop improvements and review of tradeoffs. This also allows for DDOT to evaluate, develop and unveil a range of physical and operational improvements to enhance multimodal connectivity, mobility and safety in the area.

list.Title: 1E-2025-1202 RESOLUTION ON THE RECYCLING REFUND AND LITTER REDUCTION AMENDMENT ACT OF 2025 (B26-0058)
list.Description:
ANC 1E recommends requiring a D.C.-specific imprint or scannable code on beverage
containers, in addition to the UPC code, to prevent fraudulent redemption, and direct funds from the District Recycling Fund to support small breweries and grocers with any administrative burden. ANC 1E recommends that small breweries and wineries be exempt from the 4-cent distributor fee or fully exempted from registration fees, recognizing their limited production scale and the disproportionate administrative burden such fees could impose.

list.Title: #1E-2025-0905 A RESOLUTION URGING CONGRESS TO OPPOSE THE US HOUSE COMMITTEE ON OVERSIGHT & ACCOUNTABILITY’S 16 BILLS ROLLING BACK DC SELF GOVERNANCE
list.Description:
ANC 1E strongly opposes the package of bills marked up by the U.S. House Committee
on Oversight and Accountability on September 10, 2025. These constitute an unprecedented attack on the District’s limited autonomy, local self-governance, and democratic accountability. ANC 1E condemns the harmful impacts these bills will have on the District’s criminal justice system, public safety, and core democratic institutions, recognizing that such measures would destabilize families, criminalize poverty, undermine proven safety strategies, and erode the fundamental right of District residents to govern themselves without interference. ANC 1E urges all U.S. House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate members to all legislation seeking to strip the District's authority to govern itself. ANC 1E reaffirms the right of our residents to make their own decisions. We call on the District Mayor and District Council and the Office of the Attorney General to oppose these bills and to defend home rule.

list.Title: #1E-2025-0902 RESOLUTION REQUESTING A STREATERY EXTENSION FOR WARD 1 BUSINESSES
list.Description:
That ANC 1E respectfully requests that DDOT and the Deputy Mayor for Planning and
Economic Development (DMPED) grant deadline extensions for businesses across Ward 1 that are actively pursuing compliance with the Permanent Streatery Program, including:
• Up to 6-12 month extensions allowing businesses continue operating existing streateries while
pursuing compliance by 2026, recognizing financial and operational constraints on small businesses;
• The option to submit a remediation plan with clear milestones as needed;
• Prioritization of safety and accessibility improvements (including bollards, ADA compliance, and public space fees) during any extension period.
ANC 1E urges DDOT and DMPED to recognize the especially difficult circumstances
currently facing this neighborhood, and to account for the disproportionate economic and social impacts our community is experiencing due to federal actions as they implement the Permanent Streatery Program.

list.Title: 1E-2025-0702 RESOLUTION SUPPORTING A STIPULATED LICENSE FOR REIGN RESTAURANT, LLC
list.Description: ANC 1E supports a Stipulated License to allow the Applicant to operate in accordance with the agreed upon SA during the public notice period until the SA is approved by ABCA and a license is issued.

list.Title: 1E-2025-0506: RESOLUTION OPPOSING B26-0208, THE "OPEN MEETINGS CLARIFICATION AMENDMENT ACT OF 2025
list.Description: ANC 1E opposes B26-0208 and any effort to create new exemptions to the District's Open Meetings Act that would reduce public visibility into government meetings, deliberations, or decision-making; The D.C. Council should reject B26-0208 because it removes essential guardrails that help ensure public officials remain accountable to the people they serve, and because the long-term concern it raises for trust, transparency, and public engagement far outweighs the short-term administrative flexibility it might offer; ANC 1E urges the Council of the District of Columbia to reject this bill and instead recommit to strengthening transparency protections across all levels of District government.

list.Title: 1E-2025-0504 RESOLUTION REGARDING THE NEW C61 RIDER IMPACTS UNDER THE WMATA BETTER BUS NETWORK INITIATIVE & ACCESS TO THE WASHINGTON HOSPITAL AND VETERAN’S MEDICAL CENTERS

Changes requested include: (1) Adjusting the School Zone for The Lab School, (2) Painting Crosswalks, (3) Installation of Bulb-Outs, (4) Installation of Westbound Speed Camera.

list.Title: Major Roadway Erosion 2300 Block of Nebraska Ave. (25-003359696)
list.Description: ANC 3D requests DDOT install a proper curb and gutters on the 2300 block of Nebraska Avenue (25-003359696) to stop erosion and storm water run off on the Trolley Trail.

list.Title: Dedicated Funding for Maintenance of Green Infrastructure for Stormwater
list.Description: ANC 3D requests the Mayor's support in creating dedicated funding for maintenance of green infrastructure for stormwater. Commissioners recommend the funds that DC Water already collects from customers via the “Clean Rivers Impervious Area Charge” and the “DC Government Stormwater Fee” be adjusted to cover sufficient maintenance of green infrastructure. Inclusion of this maintenance seems entirely appropriate because these green infrastructure projects are an integral part of the District’s strategy for dealing effectively with green infrastructure. This approach will take it out of the annual budget maintenance of green infrastructure and put the new infrastructure on a firm long-term financial footing.

list.Title: Plowing During Recent Snow Emergency
list.Description: Many ANC 3D residents received little or no snow plowing even many days after the snow and sleet stopped. The 311 website provided almost no help. Request the District take a special look to facilitate improvements in two-way communication.

list.Title: Holiday Work Hours at the John Thompson High School
list.Description: ANC 3D approves of the application made by DCPS to perform work during DC government holidays as part of its ongoing construction activities at the John Thompson High School if needed to maintain the project timeline.

list.Title: Relief for Claudio's Table Public Space Improvements (#485465)
list.Description: ANC 3D is asking DDOT to provide relief to the owner of Claudio's Table restaurant by reducing the scope of work to patch the roadway where the sewer connection has been made. The patch made by the restaurant's contractor was roughly 3 square feet. The DDOT inspector informed the contractor a repair of approximately 1,000 square feet was required. Commissioners feel this is excessive.

list.Title: BZA Application 21421 for 4928 Indian Lane
list.Description: ANC 3D voted in support of BZA Application 21421 for a one story rear addition at 4928 Indian Lane. Immediate neighbors have been informed.  Commissioners do not believe light and air of the neighboring properties will be affected.

list.Title: ANC 3C Letter Regarding 34th Street/Reno Road NW Pedestrian Safety Issues
list.Description: ...writing to request a formal response and update regarding long-standing safety concerns along the Reno Road/34th Street NW corridor, including adjacent intersections such as Porter Street, Ordway Street, Newark Street, and Rodman Street.

list.Title: ANC 4C Resolution to Include Elevator Improvement in Petworth  Library Renovation
list.Description: The Petworth Library renovation includes moving the youth services space (books, reading area, and programming area for young children) to the second floor, but the only way for parents with young children in a stroller/wagon/etc is to use the elevator, which is more often broken than not. The DCPL team in charge of this renovation presented at our November meeting, but when both ANC 4C and Petworth community members requested the inclusion of the elevator in the Petworth Library renovation, DCPL reps refused to consider this reasonable community input and request. Please note that this was the first community forum that DCPL held with the ANC and with the community.

list.Title: Supporting Budget Stabilization for Harriet Tubman Elementary School in the FY27 DCPS Budget
Protecting Health Coverage for Columbia Heights Families and Support for the “Keep DC Healthy” Campaign
list.Description:
ANC 1A urges the Mayor, the Department of Health Care Finance (DHCF), DC Health Benefit Exchange Authority (HBX/DC Health Link), and the DC Council, in the FY27 budget and any mid-year adjustments, to: 
1) Restore the DC Health Care Alliance to at least FY25 adult eligibility and benefit levels, including reversing the phase-down of income eligibility, lifting the moratorium on new adult applicants, and eliminating unnecessary administrative barriers that cause eligible residents to lose coverage.
2)  Protect residents losing Medicaid as they transition to the Healthy DC Plan/Basic Health Program, by aligning benefits as closely as possible with full Medicaid (including adult dental, vision, behavioral health, and long-term services and supports) and preventing gaps in care for Columbia Heights families.
3) Fund robust, linguistically and culturally competent outreach and navigation in neighborhoods like Columbia Heights, working with trusted partners (e.g., CentroNía, CARECEN, MLOV..)

list.Title: In Support of Restoring Funding for the Child and Adolescent Mobile Psychiatric  Service (ChAMPS) as a Critical School Crisis Resource
list.Description: ANC 1A urges the Mayor and Council to restore ChAMPS funding to at least $1.37–$1.4 million in FY27, a level identified by Catholic Charities and advocates as the minimum needed to sustain a functioning youth crisis team, and to make this a stable, recurring investment rather than a one-year patch.  Furthermore, ANC 1A urges the Department of Behavioral Health to ensure ChAMPS is recognized as a core part of the School Behavioral Health system, not a duplicative add-on, maintaining robust daytime and evening hours that match when school-related crises actually occur.

list.Title: FY27 Funding and Expansion of Safe Passage, Safe Blocks

DCPS Changes to Kindergarten Enrollment
list.Description:
ANC 3D has received input from many of our constituents that DCPS seems to be arbitrarily forcing
children to skip Kindergarten solely based on the day in which they were born, nothing more. This
policy is not in the best interest of children, and data would support this policy can indeed put these
children on a path of disadvantage. The decision to skip Kindergarten, or any grade for that matter,
should rest with a family in consultation with local school professionals who are familiar with the
student. We urge the Council to enact the legislation submitted by the State Board of Education on an
emergency basis.

list.Title: MacArthur Blvd./Arizona Ave., N.W., Intersection Congestion
list.Description: The intersection at MacArthur Blvd. and Arizona Ave. N.W. continues to experience serious congestion, particularly during the afternoon rush hour. Although many of the drivers are from Virginia or Maryland, the impacts fall most seriously on residents of the immediate area, who are delayed from making local trips and suffer serious cut-through traffic. ANC 3D continues to hear complaints from residents, who are growing increasingly frustrated that DDOT has not taken addressed these concerns. We implore you and DDOT to act promptly and effectively to address our and their concerns.

list.Title: Foxhall Road and Reservoir Road Intersection -  TSI Service Number 25- 00400188
list.Description: ANC 3D urges DDOT to undertake a study of the Foxhall Road and Reservoir Road intersection and nearby road configurations, as requested in TSI Service Number 25- 00400188. In particular, ANC3D requests that DDOT evaluate and, if appropriate, make several changes to the configuration of the intersection to promote vehicular and pedestrian safety.

list.Title: Parking Changes in Front of CVS
list.Description: The CVS at 4859 MacArthur Blvd has“No Parking- Entrance" signs in front of the store entrance. It is ANC 3Ds position this “Entrance” parking is no longer necessary and can easily be converted into metered parking. In addition, the future parking meters can likely be moved closer to U Street in keeping with DDOT guidelines which would allow for the addition of up to four vehicle spaces. Another option could be to move the Commercial Loading Zone where the “Entrance” is now and designate metered parking where the Commercial Loading zone exists today.

list.Title: Proposed Modifications to Arizona Ave. Bike Lane NOI#25-160-CPD
list.Description:
ANC 3D has serious concerns with the proposed reconfiguration of the Arizona bike lanes, and
requests DDOT withdraw the NOI until DDOT has fully engaged with ANC3D and the
community. If DDOT does not withdraw the NOI, ANC3D opposes the reconfiguration and
requests reinstallation of the PBL as previously installed and approved by ANC3D, with
modifications as deemed appropriate to reduce or eliminate the use of flexposts.

list.Title: Resolution to Restore Trust, Transparency, and Community Access at Fort Dupont (7B-25-06)
list.Description: Advisory Neighborhood Commission 7B calls on the D.C. Council to investigate and address long-standing issues with the Fort Dupont Construction Project, which has received over $40 million in public funding but suffered repeated delays, missed deadlines, and poor transparency. This resolution urges a full accounting of expenditures, public oversight hearings, and regular community updates. It also calls for accountability for any mismanagement and for DPR to restore and expand programming to prior levels. ANC 7B emphasizes that this project’s failures represent not only fiscal mismanagement but also an equity and fairness issue for Ward 7 residents.

list.Description: ANC 5E supports the changes outlined in NOI 25-154-CPD. In addition, ANC 5E requested additional improvements 1) Prioritize enforcement of no left turn signs through ATE cameras and/or in coordination with MPD to do traffic enforcement especially during rush hours 2) Improve signage for time-restricted turnings for driver visibility by adding signage with increased font sizes and ensure they are visible with environmental factors like sun glare at play. list.Title: 2025-003 Resolution 1600 North Capitol St NW Supporting Eminent Domain and Opposing Map Amendment for current owner at 1600 North Capitol St NW
list.Description: The resolution supports 'Title II, Subtitle L. Truxton Circle Eminent Domian Authority’ that was introduced in the Mayor’s Budget Support Act of 2026 B26-0265. For almost 20 years, the owner of the vacant lot at Lot 48 in Square 3100 (1600 North Capitol St NW) has been negligent and refused to develop housing in our community.  In 2007, a PUD was approved. Since then, the owner had requested 8 PUD extensions and purposefully allowed the last extension to expire. Now the owner is submitting a map amendment for the property. This resolution also opposes the map amendment from MU-4 to MU-7B for the current developer, Joe Mamo. For almost two decades, the owner failed to deliver the much-needed housing within our community.  The community would like to see another trusted developer assume the responsibility to build housing.

list.Title: RESOLUTION #4B-0702   Supporting the Special Exemption Request in BZA Case No. 21344    262 Carroll Street Northwest
list.Description:
The applicant in Board of Zoning and Adjustment (BZA) Case No. 21344, JNM Enterprises One, LLC, seeks special exception relief pursuant to Subtitle G § 705.2 and Subtitle X § 901.2 of the District of Columbia Zoning Regulations to reduce the required rear yard setback from fifteen feet to five feet. 

The subject property, located at 262 Carroll Street NW, in the Takoma neighborhood of Ward 4, lies directly adjacent to the Takoma Metro Station and has remained visibly deteriorated and inactive for an extended period of time.

The proposed project entails the adaptive reuse and renovation of the existing structure, preserving its footprint and contributing to the revitalization of the streetscape through contextually appropriate design elements including grooved wood wall panels, heavy timber framing, and a standing seam metal roof.

list.Title: RESOLUTION #4B-25-0701   Supporting Historic Preservation Review Board Case No. 25-322    262 Carroll St NW
list.Description:
262 Carroll Street NW has remained vacant and visibly deteriorated for an extended period, detracting from the safety, vibrancy, and historic character of the surrounding neighborhood. 

The applicant is proposing an adaptive reuse and thoughtful renovation that maintains the original footprint of the structure and incorporates historically sensitive elements, including grooved wood siding, heavy timber framing, and a standing seam metal roof that reflects the architectural language of the Takoma Historic District. 

The property lies adjacent to the Takoma Metro Station and in close proximity to neighborhood anchors such as the Takoma Library, Takoma Elementary School, and various youth- and family-serving amenities. 

The project will reactivate a key parcel in a transit-rich corridor, enhance public safety and walkability, and serve as a community asset by restoring the site to a productive and welcoming use.

list.Title: Resolution in Support of a Community Benefits Agreement for the Proposed Commanders Stadium in Ward 7
list.Description:
Resolution 7B-25-02: Resolution in Support of a Community Benefits Agreement for the Proposed Commanders Stadium in Ward 7

Advisory Neighborhood Commission 7B unanimously adopted Resolution 7B-25-02 in support of a comprehensive Community Benefits Agreement (CBA) tied to the proposed Washington Commanders stadium in Ward 7. Acknowledging both the opportunity and potential disruption the project may bring, the resolution outlines a robust set of community-centered demands—including direct economic investment, job creation and local hiring guarantees, youth and adult education funding, housing protections, environmental sustainability measures, and major transit upgrades to improve access for ANC 7B residents. It also calls for ongoing community governance, transparency, and accountability throughout the stadium development process.

list.Title: A Resolution of Support for the Neighborhood Management Authority Act of 2025
list.Description: This resolution from Advisory Neighborhood Commission 1A expresses strong support for the Neighborhood Management Authority Act of 2025, which would establish a Columbia Heights Neighborhood Management Authority to address the neighborhood's ongoing economic challenges. The resolution highlights Columbia Heights' lack of meaningful investment since 2008, resulting in deteriorated infrastructure, numerous business closures, and persistent commercial vacancies. The proposed Authority would have dedicated funding through sources like reestablished performance parking zones and alcohol tax revenues, along with powers to support businesses, manage public spaces, and coordinate city services. ANC 1A calls for a public hearing on the legislation and suggests several amendments, including term limits for board members, ensuring representation for different commercial districts, and providing greater transparency about funding sources.

requires collaborative, evidence-based, community-led, and racially equitable solutions.

list.Title: Letter - Request for Legislation to Prohibit the Reinstatement of MPD Officers Convicted of Crimes or Found in Violation of General Orders
list.Description: I write to express deep concern regarding the reinstatement of Metropolitan Police Department (MPD) officers who have been convicted of crimes or found to have violated MPD General Orders. In light of the findings from the DC Auditor’s 2022 report1, I urge the Council to enact legislation that prohibits such reinstatements to uphold the integrity of our law enforcement and ensure public trust.

list.Title: RESOLUTION #4B-25-0402 Opposing DC Council’s Emergency Legislation to Close Meetings
list.Description:
?	Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4B strongly opposes the DC Council’s emergency legislation to close government meetings and urges the Council to maintain full transparency in accordance with the principles of good governance.

?	ANC 4B calls on the Council of DC to require the Office of Open Government or the Open Government Coalition to review a written request 72 hours before any meeting can be closed to the public, followed by a written public explanation before and after a meeting is closed, including a summary of what was discussed, redacted minutes or transcript and why it needed to be private.

?	The Commission mandates that ANCs receive written notice 48 hours in advance of any closed meetings related to matters affecting their jurisdiction and allow representatives to attend closed meetings, in a non-voting capacity, to ensure hyper-local concerns are considered.

?

list.Title: RESOLUTION #4B-25-0403 Opposing the U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia’s Consideration of Withholding Police Misconduct Information from Defense Attorneys
list.Description:
?	Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4B strongly opposes any effort by the U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia to withhold police misconduct records from defense attorneys and urges the office to maintain full transparency in accordance with constitutional and ethical obligations.

?	The Commission calls on the DC Council, Mayor’s Office, DC Attorney General, and Public Defender Service for the District of Columbia to take all necessary actions to oppose any policy limiting access to police misconduct records and ensure enforcement of disclosure obligations.

list.Title: RESOLUTION #4B-25-0404 Condemning Congressional Efforts to Undermine Washington, DC’s Status as a Sanctuary City
list.Description:
?	Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4B strongly condemns any congressional attempts to force Washington, DC to cooperate with federal immigration enforcement and reaffirms our commitment to protecting the rights and safety of all residents, regardless of immigration status.

?	The Commission calls on the DC Council, Mayor, and Attorney General to vigorously defend the District’s Sanctuary City policies, resist federal overreach, and take legal action if necessary to uphold local autonomy and protect immigrant communities.

list.Title: RESOLUTION #4B-25-0405 Calling For the DC Council To Strengthen and Expand “Ban the Box” Policies
list.Description:
?	Advisory Neighborhood Commission 4B calls on the DC Council to amend and strengthen Ban the Box policies by expanding the law to private employers to ensure broad protections against hiring discrimination, ensuring that individuals convicted of violent offenses, including those who later receive presidential pardons, must undergo an additional public safety review before being considered for positions in law enforcement or government, mandating an appeals process for applicants denied employment based on criminal records, ensuring fairness in hiring decisions and providing incentives for businesses to hire returning citizens, including tax credits and workforce development funding.

?	The Commission urges the DC Council to introduce legislation that prevents law enforcement officers convicted of crimes, including those later pardoned, from being reinstated to duty or holding public safety positions.

list.Title: RESOLUTION REGARDING FACILITY CONDITIONS IN FOREST HILLS PARK REQUIRING DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA DEPARTMENT OF PARKS AND RECREATION, AND DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES ATTENTION
list.Description: ANC 3F passed a resolution urging the D.C. Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR), the Department of General Services (DGS), and other relevant agencies to address ongoing maintenance and safety concerns in Forest Hills Park. Despite a prior resolution in July 2024, many issues remain unresolved, including overflowing trash, vandalized fences, rat sightings, broken playground equipment, and unsafe conditions. The resolution calls for increased trash collection, prompt repairs, a clear timeline for completing outstanding work orders, better signage, and coordinated agency enforcement. It also reaffirms ANC 3F’s support for community-led efforts to improve and maintain the park.

list.Title: RESOLUTION SUPPORTING DDOT TAKING MEASURES  TO IMPROVE TRAFFIC SAFETY ON AND AROUND THE  FRANKLIN STREET BRIDGE
list.Description: the ANC requests DDOT move forward on common sense safety mitigations outlined in the Reed Street Development Plan, including: The addition of no left-turn signage and installation of center line hardening to prevent left turns from the (unnamed) alley just west of 10th street onto the east end of the Bridge; Installation of a more permanent solution to prevent access to the Bridge from the Reed Street Alley until Reed Street is realigned as intended in the approved PUD; Adding all-way stop signs at 10th and Evart Streets; Adding red light cameras at the intersection of 10th Street and Franklin Street NE; and Adding speed cameras on the bridge, and the Commission requests DDOT to promptly undertake a comprehensive planning process to improve the safety of the Franklin Street Bridge, to include consideration of the issues in the resolution.

list.Title: RESOLUTION SUPPORTING DDOT USING CONTRAFLOW  BIKE LANES TO PROMOTE BICYCLE INFRASTRUCTURE  CONNECTIVITY AND CYCLIST SAFETY ON LOCAL  NEIGHBORHOOD ROADS
list.Description: Advisory Neighborhood Commission 5B strongly supports that DDOT make greater use of contraflow bike lanes to promote traffic calming; enhance the safety, connectivity, and accessibility of local bike lanes and bike-friendly roads; and avoid major disruptions to traffic, parking, or other roadway uses;  ANC 5B requests contraflow bike lanes be implemented on existing one-way blocks;  ANC 5B requests one-way conversion with contraflow bike lanes for blocks where neighbors and advocates have requested such treatments;  ANC 5B calls on DDOT to continue pursuing neighborhood bikeway treatments on 13th St NE and 14th St NE, and/or release any existing work products to the public;  ANC 5B recommends DDOT pursue more neighborhood bikeway treatments in its Strategic Bike Plan work as it moves forward; and ANC 5B requests a response on these matters from DDOT representatives involved in active transportation planning.

list.Title: Resolution urging the District of Columbia government to address hazardous conditions in the alley of the 3000 and 3100 blocks of Monroe Street NE.
list.Description:
ANC 5B passed a resolution urging the District of Columbia government to address hazardous conditions in the alleyways of the 3000 and 3100 blocks of Monroe Street NE. Residents have faced over 15 years of issues including illegal dumping, crumbling infrastructure, poor drainage, and vermin infestations—posing serious health, safety, and environmental risks.

The resolution calls on eight District agencies to take coordinated action. It includes demands for stronger enforcement from DPW, infrastructure repairs by DDOT, erosion control by DC Water, and public health interventions from DOH and DOEE. The goal is a sustained, interagency response to ensure long-term improvements?

list.Title: Resolution #4b-25-0302 - Calling On the DC Department of Public Works To Enforce ROSA Violations For Vehicles With Temporary Tags
list.Description:
"?	ANC 4B urges DPW to enforce ROSA regulations on vehicles with temporary tags, ensuring that long-term parked vehicles are properly registered in the District.

?	The Commission requests increased DPW enforcement patrols in neighborhoods experiencing a high volume of out-of-state and temporary-tagged vehicles parked beyond the legal limit.


?	ANC 4B calls on the DC Council to clarify and strengthen ROSA enforcement rules, ensuring that vehicles with temporary plates cannot evade registration and parking regulations.

?	The Commission requests a quarterly report from DPW on current ROSA enforcement efforts within the boundaries of ANC 4B, including actions taken against vehicles with temporary tags, beginning March 1, 2025.

Letter Urging Policy Changes to Metropolitan Police Department General Orders to Prohibit the Reinstatement of Police Officers Convicted of Crimes
list.Description: I am writing to express our deep concern regarding the recent reinstatement of two Metropolitan Police Department (MPD) officers, Terence Sutton and Andrew Zabavsky, following their convictions related to the death of Karon Hylton-Brown. We urge MPD to draft a general order or revise existing general orders explicitly prohibiting the reinstatement of police officers who have been convicted of crimes that directly impact the communities in which they serve, regardless of subsequent pardons. Maintaining public trust in law enforcement is essential to ensure all residents' safety, accountability, and justice.

list.Title: Resolution in Support of Protecting Residents from Second-Hand Smoke in Multi- Unit Housing in Washington, DC
list.Description: Reflecting numerous pubic comments and concerns, Advisory Neighborhood Commission (ANC) 3F passed a resolution urging the DC Council and relevant agencies to consider expanding smoke-free protections to multi-unit housing in Washington, DC. The resolution highlights the health risks of second-hand smoke, its ability to transfer among units, and its negative impact on residents and property-owners. The resolution lists policies that support improvements in air quality. The resolution was approved unanimously (6-0) at the ANC 3F meeting on March 18, 2025.

list.Title: Requesting Deadline for Action on Property at 1700 30th Street, S.E. (7B-24-05)
list.Description: Resolution formally requesting that the Department of Buildings (DOB) to set a deadline of April 1, 2025 for the owner of the property at 1700 30th Street, S.E. to either take action to bring the property up to code and make it habitable, or to begin the process of demolition. And, that if the property owner does not meet the deadline set by DOB for either improvement or demolition, ANC 7B requests that DOB proceed with the demolition of the property, using any available legal mechanisms, and bill the property owner for all associated costs, including but not limited to the cost of demolition, removal of debris, and any administrative fees. Finally, that the Department of Buildings regularly monitor the property and keep the community informed of any actions taken to address the situation, ensuring that residents, particularly schoolchildren and families in the neighborhood, are protected from the ongoing hazards posed by the neglected property.

Resolution 7B-24-04 is ANC 7B's official, on the record support of DDOT’s intent outlined in NOI #24-182-TESD to lower the speed limit on Pennsylvania Avenue SE between Minnesota Avenue and Southern Avenue from 30 MPH to 25 MPH.

In addition to support of NOI 24-182-TESD, this resolution requests that DDOT also install additional speed limit signage on Pennsylvania Avenue SE between Barney Circle and Minnesota Avenue SE, and that these signs should clearly indicate that the speed limit on this stretch of Pennsylvania Avenue SE is 25 MPH in both directions, consistent with the speed limit on other sections of the corridor.

list.Title: RESOLUTION OPPOSING WMATA'S REMOVAL OF P ST NW BUS STOP
list.Description: Advisory Neighborhood Commission 5E passed Resolution No. 2024-0002 opposing WMATA's removal without public notice of a bus stop at the intersection of N Capitol St NW and P St NW. This removal affects residents and students who now have to walk through dangerous intersections at New York Ave and Florida Ave to reach the nearest stop.

list.Title: ANC 7B's Feedback & Recommendations on the 2025 Better Bus Network Proposal (7B-24-03)
list.Description: 7B-24-03 is ANC 7B's official comments on the proposed BetterBus network and is directed at both DDOT and WMATA. The resolution clearly names which suggestions are intended for WMATA (ie route and frequency) and which are directed towards DDOT (ie bus shelters and street upgrades). In addition to uploading here, we have also emailed this resolution and uploaded it to WMATA's Better Bus comment portal.

list.Title: Requesting Pedestrian Safety Improvements on Southern Avenue, S.E. (7B-24-02)
list.Description: 7B-24-02 requests safety improvements along Southern Avenue for pedestrians. This resolution details the changes we would like to see at every Southern Avenue intersection in ANC 7B's boundaries.

list.Title: ANC 6B Letter to DDOT in Support of Multimodal Street Design on Principal Arterials
list.Description: ANC 6B Supports multimodal street designs on principal arterials throughout the city and urges DDOT to fulfill its legal obligation to give "great weight" to the recommendations of ANC 3C, 3F, and 3/4G related to the redesign of Connecticut Ave. NW.
